regra: Não copiar. Converter em abertura, pergunta, objeção, follow-up ou fechamento premium.
buscar: posts de alto engajamento sobre persuasão, objeções, atendimento, negócios locais ou experiência do cliente
entrega: 1 frase curta + 1 ângulo de objeção + 1 cuidado contra agressividade

coleta.cmd: python3 /root/.openclaw/workspace/skills/rapidapi-social-learning/scripts/social_learning.py x-search --query healthcare patient scheduling experience --search-type Top --limit 5
coleta.stdout:

      "tweet_id": "2060877481457205376",
      "screen_name": "DrSuneelDhand",
      "text": "A massive part of the reason why so many patients and doctors alike have such a negative experience of healthcare can be boiled down to one simple fact:\n\nWhat needs to be at least ONE HOUR of detailed analysis and human communication, has been condensed down to 5-10 minutes of protocol driven medicine.\n\nThat’s why if you are a patient going through the regular “system”— either insurance-based in the USA or government-based in the UK— GOOD LUCK finding a healthcare experience that you are entirely happy with. I think it’s very unlikely you will ever find one unless you go outside the system.\n\nSad but true",
      "favorites": 193,

      "tweet_id": "2060768456853664219",
      "screen_name": "RiverAkitas",
      "text": "It is a blessing for her that you are there. What is lacking is time....but time is money and the schedules are booked so tight.  It is the business office that runs the schedule.  As you know, there are all the check boxes that must be clicked, dictation to be done and then the EHR generates the progress note.  The AI can then spit out suggested treatment plan.  Not much time for a provider of any kind to get to know the patient or even really observe.  Observation is, or should I say, was a key skill and it is disappearing.  \n\nIt is really hard to find a good, caring doctor or PA these days.  All my good docs retired and I haven't found one yet that impresses me just a bit.  Nurses more so.  I come from the lab background, EHR development and implementation so I have seen a lot.",
